questions.promptTex: \text{A chord } AB \text{ subtends a central angle of } 100°. \\ \text{What is the inscribed angle on the major arc?}
hints: Inscribed angle = half the central angle.

questions.promptTex: \text{An inscribed angle measures } 35°. \text{ What is the central angle on the same arc?}

questions.prompt: Two inscribed angles subtend the same chord and lie on the same arc. They must be:
hints: Both equal half of the same central angle.
Half of the same number = same number.
questions.explanation: Both inscribed angles equal half of the same central angle, so they're equal. (Same Segment Theorem.)

questions.promptTex: \text{In a circle, } \angle APB = 60° \text{ where } P \text{ is on the circle.} \\ \text{Find the arc } AB \text{ (in degrees, measured as the central angle).}

questions.prompt: If P is on the MINOR arc (opposite side from the typical inscribed-angle picture) and the central angle is 80°, what is the inscribed angle ∠APB?
hints: The inscribed angle on the major arc would be $40°$.
On the minor arc, the inscribed angle is supplementary to that — $180° - 40°$.
questions.explanation: On the minor arc the inscribed angle is supplementary to the major-arc one: $180 - 40 = 140°$.

steps.text: **Approach.** The inscribed angle on the major arc would be $40°$.
On the minor arc, the inscribed angle is supplementary to that — $180° - 40°$.
**Solution.** On the minor arc the inscribed angle is supplementary to the major-arc one: $180 - 40 = 140°$.

questions.promptTex: \text{In a circle, chord } AB \text{ subtends an inscribed angle of } 45° \text{ at } P \\ \text{and another inscribed angle at } Q. \, P, Q \text{ are on the same side.} \\ \text{What is the inscribed angle at } Q?
hints: Same Segment Theorem applies: same chord, same arc → same angle.
questions.explanation: All inscribed angles on the same arc subtending the same chord are equal. So angle at Q also equals $45°$.

steps.text: **Approach.** Same Segment Theorem applies: same chord, same arc → same angle.
**Solution.** All inscribed angles on the same arc subtending the same chord are equal. So angle at Q also equals $45°$.

questions.promptTex: \text{Quadrilateral } ABCD \text{ is inscribed in a circle. } \\ \angle A = 75°. \text{ Find } \angle C.
hints: Opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary.
questions.explanation: Cyclic quadrilateral: opposite angles sum to $180°$. So $\angle C = 180 - 75 = 105°$.

steps.text: **Cyclic quadrilateral property:** opposite angles are supplementary.

questions.promptTex: \text{In a circle, } \angle AOB = 140° \text{ (central). Point } P \text{ is on the major arc.} \\ \text{Point } Q \text{ is on the minor arc. Find } \angle APB + \angle AQB.
hints: $\angle AQB = 110°$ (supplementary to angle on other arc).
questions.explanation: $\angle APB = 70°$, $\angle AQB = 180 - 70 = 110°$. Sum $= 180°$. (Always true: angles on opposite arcs are supplementary.)

steps.text: $\angle AQB = 110°$ (supplementary to angle on other arc).
**Solution.** $\angle APB = 70°$, $\angle AQB = 180 - 70 = 110°$. Sum $= 180°$. (Always true: angles on opposite arcs are supplementary.)
